commit.message:
fix: restore MCP servers and skills when recreating expired session (#330)

## Problem
When a session's JSON-RPC connection is lost and the server reports
"Session not found" (session expired), the reconnect path in
`SendPromptAsync` creates a fresh `SessionConfig` that was missing
`McpServers` and `SkillDirectories`. This caused the "environment to
keep going away" — MCP tools and skills disappeared after reconnection.

## Root Cause
In `CopilotService.cs`, the `freshConfig` in the "Session not found"
catch block only set `Model`, `WorkingDirectory`, `Tools`, and
`OnPermissionRequest`. The original `CreateSessionAsync()` also includes
`McpServers` and `SkillDirectories` loaded from settings.

## Fix
Updated the reconnect path's `freshConfig` to load `McpServers` and
`SkillDirectories` from settings, matching the original
`CreateSessionAsync` behavior.

## Tests
Added 3 structural regression tests in `ConnectionRecoveryTests.cs`:
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_IncludesMcpServers`
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_IncludesSkillDirectories`
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_MatchesCreateSessionFields`

All 34 ConnectionRecoveryTests pass. Build succeeds on Mac Catalyst.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reduce Wayland input handler complexity

Extract event-specific handlers from HandleEvent switch statements
in pointer, keyboard, and touch input handlers. This reduces cyclomatic
complexity from cc>25 to cc=2 for all three handlers.

Changes:
- pointer.HandleEvent: cc 31 → 2 (93.5% reduction)
- keyboard.HandleEvent: cc 28 → 2 (92.9% reduction)
- touch.HandleEvent: cc 25 → 2 (92.0% reduction)

Each event case is now handled by a dedicated helper method:
- pointer.go: Added 8 methods (handleEnterEvent, handleLeaveEvent, etc.)
- keyboard.go: Added 6 methods (handleKeymapEvent, handleKeyEvent, etc.)
- touch.go: Added 5 methods (handleDownEvent, handleUpEvent, etc.)

All input tests pass (100% pass rate).
Zero functions with cc>15 in input package.

Closes: PLAN.md Step 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initialize V1 drone-platform monorepo with scaffolding and CI enhancements (#6)

* Add V1 bootstrap project scaffold

Initial bootstrap for the drone-platform V1 monorepo. Adds repository skeleton including docs and ADRs, profiles, topologies, example inventory and deployments, interface contracts, adapter and mission-manager stubs, infra skeletons (Ansible, Docker Compose), Nix dev shell (flake.nix), CI workflow, helper scripts (bootstrap and config validation), license, and README. Provides minimal validation tooling and CI wiring to ensure config model completeness and to serve as a starting point for implementing autonomy, adapters, and deployment automation.

* Update .editorconfig, ansible, scripts, and topology

Add Python section to .editorconfig to enforce 4-space indentation. Enable host_key_checking in infra/ansible/ansible.cfg. Add an extra profile validation in ops/scripts/validate-config.sh to ensure deployments/full_sitl__split_device.yaml uses profile: full_sitl. Rename rullset.json to ruleset.json (fix typo). Add telemetry_stack role binding to topologies/split_device.yaml.

* Add config validator, CI job, and infra docs

Add a Python config validator and integrate it into CI: introduce ops/scripts/validate-config.py and a new GitHub Actions workflow (config-validation.yml), update CI to install pyyaml and run the validator, and make the shell script call the new Python validator. Provide infra skeletons and docs: add ansible role placeholders, ansible site/inventory templates, docker-compose base file, and architecture docs. Update developer tooling and docs: adjust README, onboarding, and flake.nix to include pyyaml and dev tooling (ansible, docker client, docker-compose, yamllint, pre-commit). Also add a simple mission_manager stub that parses a profile scenario and expand mission/vehicle interface docs.

* Update ops/scripts/validate-config.py

commit.message:
fix: prevent stack overflow in Test262 runner

- Increase test262 thread stack from 64 MiB to 128 MiB
- Reduce MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH from 512 to 256 to limit native stack
  consumption from deeply recursive JavaScript code
- Add push_call_frame/pop_call_frame guards to dispatch_call_value and
  dispatch_call_with_this which previously bypassed depth checking,
  allowing unbounded recursion through Function.prototype.call/apply

fix: set SatelliteResourceLanguages=en to prevent FileNotFoundException on startup

When SatelliteResourceLanguages includes non-English cultures (fr/de/nl), the
Android runtime expects those satellite DLLs to exist (e.g. fr/System.Private.CoreLib.resources.dll).
These DLLs do not exist for all .NET runtime assemblies, causing FileNotFoundException.

Setting it to 'en' only makes the runtime fall back to neutral (English) resources
for all cultures gracefully. Our app handles its own localization via LocalizationService.

commit.message:
fix: replace Navigator LockManager with in-memory lock

Root cause: Supabase auth client uses navigator.locks on web for
cross-tab token synchronization. When onAuthStateChange fires during
getSession(), both compete for the same exclusive lock, producing:
  'Acquiring an exclusive Navigator LockManager lock ... timed out'

This deadlock blocks ALL subsequent Supabase API calls — explaining
why canAddVehicle() hung indefinitely and vehicle saves never reached
the server.

Fix: provide a custom in-memory promise-chain lock (webSafeLock) that
serializes auth operations without using navigator.locks. This avoids
the deadlock while still preventing concurrent auth races.

Also restores canAddVehicle tier check now that the underlying
Supabase client issue is resolved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make DEBUG_ATTACHED thread-local to fix flaky debugger tests

DEBUG_ATTACHED was a static AtomicBool shared across all threads, but
ACTIVE_DEBUGGER is thread_local. When tests run in parallel, one thread's
detach_debugger() would clear the flag for all threads, causing intermittent
test failures (test_breakpoint_hit_once, test_breakpoint_inspect_accumulator).

Changed to thread_local Cell<bool> so attach/detach is per-thread.

Fix: Virtual entity ChildList fields render as plain text input instead of sub-grid editor (#1365)

* Initial plan

* fix: virtual entity ChildList fields render as sub-grid in VNext SPA

- BuildEntitySchema now maps FormFieldType.ChildList to "CustomHtml" type
  so the VNext SPA's renderFormField dispatches to renderSubListEditor
  instead of falling through to a plain text input.
- BuildSubFieldSchemas now has a metadata-driven path for virtual entity
  ChildList fields: when IsChildListType fails but ChildEntitySlug is set,
  it uses the registered child entity DataEntityMetadata to build the same
  Dictionary<string,object?> sub-field schema as the CLR-based path.
- New BuildSubFieldSchemasFromEntity helper covers lookup, enum, calculated
  and copyFromParent metadata for virtual-entity child fields.
- BuildViewRowsHtml now renders virtual entity ChildList fields as a
  read-only HTML table via new BuildMetadataChildListViewHtml helper,
  instead of showing raw JSON.
- Updated RouteRegistrationExtensionsTests to assert "CustomHtml" type
  and non-empty subFields (previously allowed ChildList as fallback).
- Added ExportTests.BuildSubFieldSchemas_ForVirtualEntityChildList_ReturnsSubFieldsFromChildMeta.

Co-authored-by: WillEastbury <10882286+WillEastbury@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate Dictionary<string,string> allocations in child list view rendering

Replace ParseListOfStringDicts (allocates Dictionary per row) with direct
JsonDocument.Parse + TryGetProperty. Lookup resolution uses linear scan
over LookupOptions instead of materializing a HashMap per cell.

commit.message:
Fix span attributes lost when Azure Monitor distro sampler drops constructor attrs (#341)

## Problem

When users initialize `AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer` with
`configure_azure_monitor()` (the recommended Azure Monitor distro path),
**all `gen_ai.*` span attributes are silently lost** from Application
Insights. The `customDimensions` field contains only
`_MS.ResourceAttributeId`, and the dependency Type column shows "N/A".

## Root Cause

The OTel Python SDK's `Tracer.start_span()` passes user-provided
`attributes` to the sampler as input, but only copies
`SamplingResult.attributes` onto the created span ([`opentelemetry-sdk
trace/__init__.py` line
1168](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/blob/main/opentelemetry-sdk/src/opentelemetry/sdk/trace/__init__.py#L1168)):

```python
span = _Span(
    ...
    attributes=sampling_result.attributes.copy(),  # Only sampler attrs, NOT user attrs!
)
```

Samplers like the Azure Monitor distro's `RateLimitedSampler` (default
when using `configure_azure_monitor()`) do not return user attributes in
their `SamplingResult`. Attributes set via `span.set_attribute()` after
creation work fine because they write directly to `span._attributes`.

## Evidence

| Setup | `customDimensions` has `gen_ai.*` | Type column |
|-------|-----------------------------------|-------------|
| `configure_azure_monitor()` (distro) | ❌ Empty | N/A |
| Manual `TracerProvider` + `AzureMonitorTraceExporter` | ✅ All present
| azure_openai |
| `configure_azure_monitor()` + `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=always_on` | ✅ All
present | azure_openai |

## Fix

After `start_span()`, explicitly re-apply all attributes via
`set_attribute()`. The attributes are still passed to `start_span()` for
sampler input, but the `set_attribute()` calls guarantee they survive
regardless of sampler implementation.

## Affected Attributes

- `gen_ai.operation.name` (invoke_agent, chat, execute_tool)
- `gen_ai.provider.name` (shows "N/A" in Azure Monitor Type column)
- `gen_ai.agent.name` / `gen_ai.agent.id`
- `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` / `output_tokens`
- `gen_ai.request.model` / `gen_ai.response.model`
- All other attributes passed to `_start_span()`

## Test

Added
`test_start_span_attributes_survive_sampler_that_drops_constructor_attrs`
with a `DroppingMockTracer` that simulates a sampler discarding
constructor attributes. All 96 existing tests continue to pass.

commit.message:
Per-connection CSP nonce pool, TLS pre-warm, drop Vary header

- ConnectionNoncePool: 2048-slot struct array, ordinal-indexed, one RNG
  call per connection instead of per request
- TlsConnectionMiddleware: Rent/Return nonce slots on connect/disconnect,
  set IConnectionNonceFeature for request-level lookup
- GetCspNonce: check connection feature first (O(1)), fallback to RNG
- WarmUpTls: loopback TLS handshake to pre-warm OpenSSL state machine
- ClientCertificateRequired = false on SslServerAuthenticationOptions
- Expose BmwContext.Features for connection-level feature access
- Remove Vary: Accept-Encoding from static file responses
